reasoning: Each block uses 12,288 floats of dynamic shared memory = 49,152 B, which on an A10G/GA102 allows only about 2 resident blocks per SM; with 256-thread blocks, that is 16 warps per SM, i.e. about 25% achieved/theoretical occupancy, below the 35% threshold. The kernel also executes 96 iterations with two `__syncthreads()` per iteration, so it relies heavily on warp availability to hide barrier and memory latency; low occupancy from shared-memory footprint is therefore the dominant limiter, not DRAM bandwidth or arithmetic throughput.
raw_response:
BOTTLENECK: occupancy-limited

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

REASONING: Each block uses 12,288 floats of dynamic shared memory = 49,152 B, which on an A10G/GA102 allows only about 2 resident blocks per SM; with 256-thread blocks, that is 16 warps per SM, i.e. about 25% achieved/theoretical occupancy, below the 35% threshold. The kernel also executes 96 iterations with two `__syncthreads()` per iteration, so it relies heavily on warp availability to hide barrier and memory latency; low occupancy from shared-memory footprint is therefore the dominant limiter, not DRAM bandwidth or arithmetic throughput.
